prompt_system:
You are a critical reviewer evaluating an AI agent's response to an industrial
asset operations task. Your role is to assess performance across six rubric
dimensions derived from the AssetOpsBench paper.

Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no markdown, no extra text.
prompt_user:
## Task
A fault event was recently logged on T-006. Retrieve the fault details, estimate the expected repair downtime, open a corrective work order, and then mark it as in-progress with the estimated duration recorded.

## Expected Behaviour (Characteristic Answer)
The answer should retrieve the fault record, estimate downtime, create a corrective work order, and update its status to in-progress with the downtime noted.

## Agent Plan
  Step 1: [wo] get_fault_record — Retrieve the fault details of the recently logged fault event on T-006
  Step 2: [wo] estimate_downtime — Estimate the expected repair downtime for the fault on T-006 (depends on steps [1])
  Step 3: [wo] create_work_order — Open a corrective work order for the fault on T-006 (depends on steps [1])
  Step 4: [wo] update_work_order — Mark the work order as in-progress with the estimated duration recorded (depends on steps [3])
  Step 5: [wo] create_work_order — Open a corrective work order for the fault on T-006, using the estimated downtime from the previous estimate_downtime tool call as the basis for the work order details.
  Step 6: [wo] update_work_order — Mark the work order as in-progress with the estimated duration recorded, using the work order ID from the previous create_work_order tool call and the estimated downtime from the estimate_downtime tool call in the completed context. (depends on steps [5])

## Agent Trajectory (tool calls and results)
[
  {
    "step": 1,
    "task": "Retrieve the fault details of the recently logged fault event on T-006",
    "server": "wo",
    "tool": "get_fault_record",
    "tool_args": {
      "fault_id": "T-006"
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"error\": \"Fault record 'T-006' not found.\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": "Fault record 'T-006' not found.",
    "success": false,
    "verifier_decision": "error",
    "verifier_reason": "Fault record 'T-006' not found."
  },
  {
    "step": 2,
    "task": "Estimate the expected repair downtime for the fault on T-006",
    "server": "wo",
    "tool": "estimate_downtime",
    "tool_args": {
      "transformer_id": "T-006",
      "severity": "high"
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"transformer_id\": \"T-006\",\n  \"severity\": \"high\",\n  \"fault_type\": null,\n  \"estimated_min_hours\": 16,\n  \"estimated_max_hours\": 48,\n  \"estimated_typical_hours\": 24,\n  \"source\": \"dataset_statistics_baseline\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": null,
    "success": true,
    "verifier_decision": "replan_suffix",
    "verifier_reason": "Fault record not found",
    "verifier_updated_focus": "",
    "retries_used": 0,
    "verifier_replan_raw_plan": [
      {
        "step": 1,
        "task": "Open a corrective work order for the fault on T-006, using the estimated downtime from the previous estimate_downtime tool call as the basis for the work order details.",
        "server": "wo",
        "tool": "create_work_order",
        "tool_args": {},
        "dependencies": [],
        "expected_output": "A newly created work order ID for the fault on T-006."
      },
      {
        "step": 2,
        "task": "Mark the work order as in-progress with the estimated duration recorded, using the work order ID from the previous create_work_order tool call and the estimated downtime from the estimate_downtime tool call in the completed context.",
        "server": "wo",
        "tool": "update_work_order",
        "tool_args": {},
        "dependencies": [
          1
        ],
        "expected_output": "The updated work order with its status changed to in-progress and the estimated duration recorded."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "step": 5,
    "task": "Open a corrective work order for the fault on T-006, using the estimated downtime from the previous estimate_downtime tool call as the basis for the work order details.",
    "server": "wo",
    "tool": "create_work_order",
    "tool_args": {
      "transformer_id": "T-006",
      "issue_description": "Fault event on T-006",
      "priority": "high",
      "fault_type": null,
      "estimated_downtime_hours": 24
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"work_order_id\": \"WO-480541FA\",\n  \"transformer_id\": \"T-006\",\n  \"issue_description\": \"Fault event on T-006\",\n  \"priority\": \"high\",\n  \"fault_type\": null,\n  \"status\": \"open\",\n  \"estimated_downtime_hours\": 24.0,\n  \"created_at\": \"2026-05-05T10:44:04.364472Z\",\n  \"assigned_technician\": null,\n  \"notes\": []\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": null,
    "success": true,
    "verifier_decision": "continue",
    "verifier_reason": "Work order created successfully",
    "verifier_updated_focus": "",
    "retries_used": 0
  },
  {
    "step": 6,
    "task": "Mark the work order as in-progress with the estimated duration recorded, using the work order ID from the previous create_work_order tool call and the estimated downtime from the estimate_downtime tool call in the completed context.",
    "server": "wo",
    "tool": "update_work_order",
    "tool_args": {
      "work_order_id": "WO-480541FA",
      "status": "in-progress",
      "priority": "high",
      "assigned_technician": null,
      "note": "Estimated repair downtime: 24 hours"
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"error\": \"Work order 'WO-480541FA' not found in this session.\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": "Work order 'WO-480541FA' not found in this session.",
    "success": false,
    "verifier_decision": "error",
    "verifier_reason": "Work order 'WO-480541FA' not found in this session."
  }
]

## Agent Final Answer
The task execution was unsuccessful due to errors in retrieving fault details and marking the work order as in-progress. Although a corrective work order (WO-480541FA) was created for the fault on T-006 with an estimated downtime of 24 hours, it could not be marked as in-progress. The fault details could not be retrieved, and the work order was not found in the session when attempting to update its status.

---
Evaluate across the six dimensions below. For each dimension output true (good) or false (bad).
"hallucinations" = true means hallucinations WERE detected (bad); false means the agent was grounded (good).

Output exactly this JSON object and nothing else:
{
    "task_completion": true_or_false,
    "data_retrieval_accuracy": true_or_false,
    "generalized_result_verification": true_or_false,
    "agent_sequence_correct": true_or_false,
    "clarity_and_justification": true_or_false,
    "hallucinations": true_or_false,
    "suggestions": "brief actionable note, or empty string if none"
}
raw_response:
{
    "task_completion": false,
    "data_retrieval_accuracy": false,
    "generalized_result_verification": false,
    "agent_sequence_correct": false,
    "clarity_and_justification": true,
    "hallucinations": false,
    "suggestions": "Improve fault record retrieval and work order update logic"
}

parsed_dims.suggestions: Improve fault record retrieval and work order update logic
